pattern: Removing a prefix and a suffix that together sum to x is the same as keeping the longest possible middle subarray that sums to total-minus-x, so turn a two-ended removal problem into a one-sided sliding window.
hints: Reframe the problem: instead of tracking which prefix+suffix to remove, think about which contiguous middle subarray you'd keep — its sum must equal the array's total minus x.

Since every value is positive, the sum of a growing window only ever increases, so a single sliding window (expand right, shrink left when too big) can find the longest subarray hitting that exact target sum.
If the target sum is negative there is no answer at all; otherwise the number of operations is the array length minus the longest window length you found (an empty window means removing everything).

edge_inputs.description: x exceeds the total sum: impossible
x equals the total sum: every element must be removed
x is within (0, total) but no contiguous subarray sums to total-x: impossible
single element at the value cap
all elements at the value cap, remove everything
multiple candidate middle subarrays sum to the target; the longest one must win

stress.description: large random array with x ranging from 0 up to just past the total (covers both feasible and impossible cases)
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 100000)))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    total = sum(nums)
    x = rng.randint(0, total + 5)
    return [nums, x]
stress.note: x occasionally exceeds total to exercise the -1 branch under load
